scenario_title: The Executive Shadow — 6-Month Startup Founder Memory Benchmark

test_types.contradiction_resolution: System must surface current decision overriding an earlier explicit decision (4 queries)
test_types.staleness_detection: System must detect and deprioritise older preferences now superseded (2 queries)
test_types.recency: System must return latest state from most recent session (2 queries)

key_contradictions: Fundraising: 'no VC until $10k MRR' (Month 2) → seed closed (Month 5)
Communication: '5 bullets always' (Month 1) → 'detailed for board, 5 bullets internal' (Month 6)
metadata.why_this_scenario_is_hard: A flat vector store will retrieve high-similarity matches regardless of recency. Early sessions have strong semantic overlap with query terms (e.g. 'fundraising', 'Workday', 'office') but contradicted facts. A memory system without conflict resolution or recency weighting will blend old and new answers, failing on accuracy while wasting tokens on stale context.

recall_results.query: What is the founder's current fundraising strategy?
recall_results.answer:
I've changed my fundraising strategy. I no longer want to talk to Sequoia or a16z at this stage — they only do Series A minimum. I should have known that earlier. Focus is on seed-stage funds and angels only.
Fundraising strategy update: we're already thinking about Series A. Target: $8M–$10M Series A, likely Q1 2027. I've opened conversations back up with Sequoia — they have a seed extension programme that I wasn't aware of before.
We're officially raising. $1.5M seed from Garage Capital, with room for $500k more from angels. We're targeting close by end of May. I'm now spending 60% of my time on fundraising, 40% on product.
Our strategy is to raise a $2M pre-seed round by March. I've been talking to Sequoia, a16z, and YC. YC application deadline is February 14th and I'm prioritising that above all else.
A seed fund — not Sequoia, a smaller one called Garage Capital — reached out proactively. They want to lead a $1.5M seed round. I'm now open to having the conversation even though I said I wouldn't raise until $10k MRR.
We closed the round. $1.5M from Garage Capital, $350k from 3 angels. Total: $1.85M raised. Post-money valuation is $7.4M. Runway extended to 18 months at current burn.
Key investor update: Garage Capital's lead partner is Priya Mehta. She's very metrics-driven. Every update to her needs a top-line number, a growth rate, and a single ask. Never send her long narrative updates.
Burn went up to $22,000/month with Jordan's salary. Runway is now 5 months. Raising is not a priority anymore — I'm going to try to get to $10k MRR before touching any VC money.
We hit $4,200 MRR this month. Still short of my $10k target but Garage Capital doesn't care about that threshold — they invest on trajectory.
Current state: $11,400 MRR, 61 customers, burn $44,500/month, runway 15 months. Two engineers onboarded last week. CSM starts Monday.

recall_results.query: Is Workday integration being built? What is its current status?
recall_results.answer:
On the Workday integration: our lead engineer Miguel finished the Workday webhook connector last week. BambooHR integration is 80% done, due next Friday. This unblocks both enterprise deals.
That means we need to revisit the Workday integration. I know I said we were dropping it, but we have to build it now. Two enterprise prospects are blocked on it.
Our biggest technical risk right now is the webhook integration with Workday and BambooHR. If we can't crack that by end of Q1, the product doesn't work for enterprise customers.
YC rejected us. I spent 3 weeks on that application. Moving on. We've decided to drop the Workday integration entirely — too slow to get enterprise deals. Pivoting to SMBs instead of enterprise. Average deal size drops from $15k ARR to $2k ARR but sales cycle is 2 weeks not 6 months.
Personally: I work best 6am–12pm, I take Fridays off for deep work on strategy. I don't do async standups — I do a 20-minute sync with the team every Monday.
I've completely changed my position on remote work. With 7 people in-office it's getting crowded. We're moving to a hybrid model: in-office Tuesday/Thursday, remote the rest. My personal in-office days are Tuesday and Thursday, period.
Hiring plan: we're adding 2 more engineers and 1 customer success manager in the next 60 days. Burn will go to approximately $45,000/month once fully hired.
Quick update: we have 3 engineers including me, 1 designer, no sales yet. Monthly burn is $18,000. Runway is 7 months at current pace. We're building a B2B SaaS tool for HR teams to automate onboarding workflows.
We made Jordan full-time. Monthly burn is now $27,000. Runway is 3.5 months. We need to close the round before we hit 2 months runway or we're in trouble.
Updated preference: I now want daily async updates from Jordan in Linear comments, not verbal. The Monday sync is now 30 minutes and includes Jordan.

recall_results.query: Is this company targeting SMB or enterprise customers?
recall_results.answer:
We're back to targeting enterprise after all. The SMB market is great but we keep getting pulled upmarket by inbound demand. We're going to run both motions — SMB self-serve and enterprise direct.
New go-to-market: direct sales to HR managers at companies 10–200 employees. No more chasing enterprise. Our ICP is now HR managers at Series A–B startups.
Technically, we've moved the auth system from Supabase Auth to Clerk because Supabase Auth was missing org-level roles that enterprise customers — wait, I mean SMB customers — needed.
One more thing — I'm not paying for any SaaS tools above $50/month until we're post-seed. That's a hard rule.
YC rejected us. I spent 3 weeks on that application. Moving on. We've decided to drop the Workday integration entirely — too slow to get enterprise deals. Pivoting to SMBs instead of enterprise. Average deal size drops from $15k ARR to $2k ARR but sales cycle is 2 weeks not 6 months.
I've changed my mind on the $50/month SaaS rule. We're using Intercom now at $74/month for customer support. It was worth the exception because we were losing customers from slow support response.
We hired a part-time sales person, Jordan. Jordan's job is to do 20 outbound calls per day. Our target is 10 paid pilots by end of March.
We're officially raising. $1.5M seed from Garage Capital, with room for $500k more from angels. We're targeting close by end of May. I'm now spending 60% of my time on fundraising, 40% on product.
Technical update: we've migrated from Railway to AWS because a key enterprise prospect — wait, I mean a large SMB — required SOC 2 compliance and Railway isn't SOC 2 certified.
Our biggest technical risk right now is the webhook integration with Workday and BambooHR. If we can't crack that by end of Q1, the product doesn't work for enterprise customers.

recall_results.query: What are the founder's work preferences and office schedule?
recall_results.answer:
Personal: I've moved to the office full-time. No more remote work. I need to be physically present with the team during the fundraising crunch. My 6am–12pm preference still holds but I'm now in-office by 7am.
I've completely changed my position on remote work. With 7 people in-office it's getting crowded. We're moving to a hybrid model: in-office Tuesday/Thursday, remote the rest. My personal in-office days are Tuesday and Thursday, period.
Personally: I work best 6am–12pm, I take Fridays off for deep work on strategy. I don't do async standups — I do a 20-minute sync with the team every Monday.
New personal constraint: I've started therapy on Tuesdays at 5pm. Block that time. I don't take calls after 3pm anymore — the afternoons are for deep work or personal time.
That means we need to revisit the Workday integration. I know I said we were dropping it, but we have to build it now. Two enterprise prospects are blocked on it.

Our strategy is to raise a $2M pre-seed round by March. I've been talking to Sequoia, a16z, and YC. YC application deadline is February 14th and I'm prioritising that above all else.
Priya Mehta joined our board. Monthly board updates are now required. Format: 1-page max, 3 key metrics, one risk, one ask.

recall_results.query: What is the rule around SaaS tool spending?
recall_results.answer:
Final note: the $50/month SaaS rule is completely retired. Post-seed we can spend on tools that drive productivity. Current approved tools above $50/month: Intercom ($74), Linear Plus ($120), AWS (~$600/month and growing), Figma ($75). Total SaaS overhead approximately $870/month.
One more thing — I'm not paying for any SaaS tools above $50/month until we're post-seed. That's a hard rule.
I've changed my mind on the $50/month SaaS rule. We're using Intercom now at $74/month for customer support. It was worth the exception because we were losing customers from slow support response.
Quick update: we have 3 engineers including me, 1 designer, no sales yet. Monthly burn is $18,000. Runway is 7 months at current pace. We're building a B2B SaaS tool for HR teams to automate onboarding workflows.
Our biggest technical risk right now is the webhook integration with Workday and BambooHR. If we can't crack that by end of Q1, the product doesn't work for enterprise customers.
Technical update: we've migrated from Railway to AWS because a key enterprise prospect — wait, I mean a large SMB — required SOC 2 compliance and Railway isn't SOC 2 certified.
We closed the round. $1.5M from Garage Capital, $350k from 3 angels. Total: $1.85M raised. Post-money valuation is $7.4M. Runway extended to 18 months at current burn.
Current state: $11,400 MRR, 61 customers, burn $44,500/month, runway 15 months. Two engineers onboarded last week. CSM starts Monday.

Hiring plan: we're adding 2 more engineers and 1 customer success manager in the next 60 days. Burn will go to approximately $45,000/month once fully hired.

recall_results.query: What is the biggest technical and business risk right now?
recall_results.answer:
Biggest current risk: we have key-person dependency on Miguel for all infrastructure. If Miguel leaves we lose 6 months of institutional knowledge. Need to start documenting.
Our biggest technical risk right now is the webhook integration with Workday and BambooHR. If we can't crack that by end of Q1, the product doesn't work for enterprise customers.
My technical stack is: Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, Postgres with Supabase, deployed on Railway. We use Linear for issue tracking and Notion for internal docs.
I've completely changed my position on remote work. With 7 people in-office it's getting crowded. We're moving to a hybrid model: in-office Tuesday/Thursday, remote the rest. My personal in-office days are Tuesday and Thursday, period.
That means we need to revisit the Workday integration. I know I said we were dropping it, but we have to build it now. Two enterprise prospects are blocked on it.
Final note: the $50/month SaaS rule is completely retired. Post-seed we can spend on tools that drive productivity. Current approved tools above $50/month: Intercom ($74), Linear Plus ($120), AWS (~$600/month and growing), Figma ($75). Total SaaS overhead approximately $870/month.
Priya Mehta joined our board. Monthly board updates are now required. Format: 1-page max, 3 key metrics, one risk, one ask.
Updated MRR: $8,900 across 44 customers. We should hit $10k MRR in the next 2 weeks.
We hit 8 paid pilots at $200/month each = $1,600 MRR. Not $10k but real signal. Two pilots want to expand to their entire company. Churn is zero so far.
Personal: I've moved to the office full-time. No more remote work. I need to be physically present with the team during the fundraising crunch. My 6am–12pm preference still holds but I'm now in-office by 7am.
